title: Multiply by 11 Trick
problem: What is 47 x 11?
cheatsheet: To multiply any 2-digit number by 11: add the two digits, place the sum between them. If sum > 9, carry the 1 to the left digit.
steps: Since 11 > 9, put 1 in the middle and add 1 to the first digit

title: Square Numbers Ending in 5
problem: What is 35 squared?
cheatsheet: For numbers ending in 5: multiply the tens digit by (tens digit + 1), then append 25.

title: Subtract from 1000
problem: What is 1000 - 467?
cheatsheet: To subtract from 1000: subtract each digit from 9 (except last from 10).

title: Doubling and Halving
problem: What is 25 x 48?
cheatsheet: Double one factor and halve the other. Keep going until one becomes easy to work with.

title: Percentages via Fractions
problem: What is 15% of 80?
cheatsheet: Break down percentages: 15% = 10% + 5%. 10% is easy (move decimal), then halve for 5%.

title: Multiplying by 5
problem: What is 86 x 5?
cheatsheet: To multiply by 5: multiply by 10 then divide by 2. Or: divide by 2 then multiply by 10.

title: Square of Numbers Near 100
problem: What is 97 squared?
cheatsheet: For numbers near 100: find the difference from 100, subtract it from the number, then square the difference and append.

title: Adding Large Numbers Left to Right
problem: What is 458 + 679?
cheatsheet: Add from left to right in chunks rather than right to left with carrying.

title: Divisibility by 9
problem: Is 837 divisible by 9?
cheatsheet: A number is divisible by 9 if the sum of its digits is divisible by 9.

title: Multiply by 9 Using Fingers
problem: What is 9 x 7?
cheatsheet: Hold up 10 fingers, fold down the 7th finger. Left fingers = tens, right fingers = ones.
workedExample: 9 x 7: fold 7th finger. Left = 6, right = 3. Answer: 63
steps: Hold up both hands (10 fingers total)
Count fingers to the left: 6 (tens digit)
Count fingers to the right: 3 (ones digit)

title: Quick Square Roots Estimation
problem: Estimate the square root of 70.
cheatsheet: Find the nearest perfect squares and estimate between them. sqrt(64)=8, sqrt(81)=9.
workedExample: sqrt(70): between 64(8) and 81(9). 70 is 6/17 of the way from 64 to 81. ~8.37

title: Multiplying by 25
problem: What is 36 x 25?
cheatsheet: To multiply by 25: multiply by 100 then divide by 4.

title: Difference of Squares
problem: What is 52 x 48?
cheatsheet: For numbers equidistant from a round number: a^2 - b^2 = (a+b)(a-b). Here: 50^2 - 2^2.

title: Calculate 15% Tip Mentally
problem: What is a 15% tip on $68?
cheatsheet: 10% + 5%. Move decimal for 10%, halve for 5%, add both.

title: Multiply Two 2-Digit Numbers
problem: What is 23 x 14?
cheatsheet: Use the distributive property: (a+b)(c+d) = ac + ad + bc + bd, grouping for easy mental math.

title: Cube Root Estimation
problem: What is the cube root of 250?

title: Adding a Series of Numbers
problem: What is the sum of 1 + 2 + 3 + ... + 100?
cheatsheet: Use the formula: n(n+1)/2 or pair first and last: (1+100) + (2+99) + ... = 101 x 50.

title: Quick Division by 4
problem: What is 332 / 4?
cheatsheet: To divide by 4: halve, then halve again.

title: Recognize Multiples of 11
problem: Is 528 divisible by 11?
cheatsheet: Alternating sum rule: add/subtract alternating digits. If result is divisible by 11, so is the number.

title: Multiply by 101
problem: What is 67 x 101?
cheatsheet: To multiply by 101: write the number twice. 67 x 101 = 6767.
steps: Simply write the number followed by itself

title: Fast Cubes
problem: What is 12 cubed?

title: Divide by 8
problem: What is 240 / 8?
cheatsheet: Divide by 2 three times, or divide by 4 then by 2.

title: Multiplying by 99
problem: What is 45 x 99?
cheatsheet: Multiply by 100 then subtract the number. 45 x 99 = 4500 - 45.

title: Fractions to Decimals
problem: What is 7/8 as a decimal?

title: Adding Fractions Quickly
problem: What is 1/3 + 1/6?
cheatsheet: Find a common denominator mentally. 1/3 = 2/6, so 2/6 + 1/6 = 3/6 = 1/2.

title: Percentage of Any Number
problem: What is 8% of 250?
cheatsheet: Find 1% (move decimal two places) then multiply. 1% of 250 = 2.5, 8% = 2.5 x 8.

title: Sales Tax Calculation
problem: A $45 item has 8.25% sales tax. What is the total cost?
cheatsheet: Total = Price x (1 + tax rate). Or: find the tax amount and add to original.

title: Discount Calculation
problem: A $120 jacket is on sale for 35% off. What is the sale price?
cheatsheet: Sale price = Original x (1 - discount). Or: find 65% of original (remaining).

title: Markup Calculation
problem: A store buys items at $20 and marks them up 60%. What is the selling price?
cheatsheet: Selling price = Cost x (1 + markup percentage).

title: Compound Percentage Change
problem: A population of 10,000 grows 10% then decreases 10%. What is the final population?
cheatsheet: Apply each change sequentially. Net effect is NOT zero when changes differ in base.

title: Finding the Original Price
problem: After a 25% discount, you paid $60. What was the original price?
cheatsheet: Sale price = Original x (1 - discount). Original = Sale / (1 - discount).

title: Percentage Increase
problem: Stock price went from $40 to $50. What is the percentage increase?
cheatsheet: % increase = (New - Old) / Old x 100. Always divide by the ORIGINAL value.

title: Percentage Decrease
problem: A team's wins dropped from 80 to 60. What is the percentage decrease?
cheatsheet: % decrease = (Old - New) / Old x 100.

title: Comparing Percentages
problem: Which is better: 20% off $100 or $15 off $80?
cheatsheet: Calculate the final price for each option, then compare.
workedExample: Option A: 100-20=80. Option B: 80-15=65. Option B saves $15 more.

title: Successive Discounts
problem: A $200 item has 40% off, then an extra 25% off. What is the final price?
cheatsheet: Apply discounts sequentially: multiply by (1-d1) then (1-d2).

title: What Percent Is X of Y?
problem: What percent is 15 of 60?
cheatsheet: X is what percent of Y = (X/Y) x 100.

title: Tip Calculation
problem: Your restaurant bill is $85. What is an 18% tip?
cheatsheet: Find 10% (move decimal), find 8% (0.8 x 10%), add both.

title: Reverse Percentage
problem: A salary increased by 12% to $56,000. What was the original salary?
cheatsheet: New = Old x 1.12. Old = New / 1.12.
steps: The new salary is 112% of the original

title: Tax-Included Price
problem: An item costs $53.60 after 7% tax. What was the pre-tax price?
cheatsheet: Total = Pre-tax x 1.07. Pre-tax = Total / 1.07.

title: Annual vs Monthly Rate
problem: An annual interest rate is 6%. What is the equivalent monthly rate (simple)?
cheatsheet: Monthly rate = Annual rate / 12.

title: Grade Average
problem: Scores: 85, 92, 78, 95, 88. What is the average (mean)?
cheatsheet: Mean = sum of all values / number of values.

title: Percentage Error
problem: You estimated 150, actual was 180. What is the percentage error?
cheatsheet: % error = |estimated - actual| / actual x 100.

title: Profit Margin
problem: Revenue $500, cost $350. What is the profit margin percentage?
cheatsheet: Profit margin = (Revenue - Cost) / Revenue x 100.

title: Appreciation Rate
problem: A house was worth $200,000 and is now worth $260,000. What is the appreciation rate?
cheatsheet: Appreciation = (New - Old) / Old x 100.

title: Ratio to Percentage
problem: The ratio of wins to total games is 3:5. What is the winning percentage?
cheatsheet: Convert ratio to fraction: 3/5 = 60%.
steps: Ratio 3:5 means 3 wins out of 5 games

title: Basic Distance-Speed-Time
problem: A car travels at 55 mph for 3.5 hours. How far does it go?
cheatsheet: Distance = Speed x Time (D = S x T)

title: Finding Speed
problem: A runner covers 10 km in 50 minutes. What is the speed in km/h?
cheatsheet: Speed = Distance / Time. Convert minutes to hours first: 50 min = 5/6 hour.

title: Finding Time
problem: A cyclist travels 45 miles at 15 mph. How long does the trip take?
cheatsheet: Time = Distance / Speed.

title: Average Speed (Harmonic Mean)
problem: Drive 60 km at 30 km/h, then 60 km at 60 km/h. What is the average speed?
cheatsheet: Average speed = Total distance / Total time. Do NOT just average the speeds!

title: Two Objects Moving Toward Each Other
problem: Two cars start 300 miles apart and drive toward each other at 50 and 70 mph. When do they meet?
cheatsheet: Combined speed = sum of individual speeds. Time = distance / combined speed.

title: Work Rate Problem
problem: Pipe A fills a tank in 6 hours. Pipe B fills it in 4 hours. How long working together?
cheatsheet: Combined rate = 1/A + 1/B. Time = 1 / (combined rate).
steps: Pipe A rate: 1/6 tank per hour
Pipe B rate: 1/4 tank per hour

title: Catch-Up Problem
problem: Runner A starts at 8 mph. 30 min later, Runner B starts at 10 mph. How long for B to catch A?
cheatsheet: Head start distance = speed_A x delay. Closing speed = speed_B - speed_A. Time = head start / closing speed.
workedExample: Head start: 8 x 0.5 = 4 miles. Closing: 10-8=2 mph. Time: 4/2 = 2 hours
steps: A's head start: 8 mph x 0.5 hr = 4 miles
Time for B to close gap: 4/2 = 2 hours

title: Round Trip Average Speed
problem: A plane flies 2000 km at 500 km/h and returns at 400 km/h. Average speed?
cheatsheet: Average = 2 x v1 x v2 / (v1 + v2) for equal distances.

title: Speed Conversion
problem: Convert 100 km/h to miles per hour. (1 km = 0.6214 mi)
cheatsheet: Multiply km/h by 0.6214 to get mph.

title: Three Workers Problem
problem: A can do a job in 10 days, B in 15 days, C in 30 days. Together?
cheatsheet: Combined rate = 1/A + 1/B + 1/C. Time = 1 / combined rate.

title: Unit Rate
problem: If 8 lb of apples cost $12, what is the cost per pound?
cheatsheet: Unit rate = Total cost / Total quantity.
steps: Divide total cost by total weight

title: Upstream/Downstream
problem: A boat goes 20 km/h in still water. Current is 4 km/h. How long for 48 km upstream?
cheatsheet: Upstream speed = boat speed - current. Downstream speed = boat + current.

title: Fuel Consumption Rate
problem: A car uses 8 gallons for 200 miles. How many gallons for a 350-mile trip?
cheatsheet: Find unit rate (gallons/mile), then multiply by distance.

title: Rate of Production
problem: A machine produces 24 units in 3 hours. How many units in 8 hours?
cheatsheet: Find rate: units/time, then multiply by new time.

title: Two Trains Same Direction
problem: Train A (80 km/h) is ahead of Train B (100 km/h) by 40 km. How long for B to catch A?
cheatsheet: Closing speed = faster - slower. Time = gap / closing speed.

title: Walking and Running
problem: You walk at 5 km/h for 2 km, then run at 10 km/h for 3 km. Total time?
cheatsheet: Time = distance/speed for each segment, then sum.

title: Filling and Emptying
problem: A tap fills at 8 L/min, drain empties at 5 L/min. Tank holds 90L and has 30L. How long to fill?
cheatsheet: Net rate = fill rate - drain rate. Time = remaining / net rate.

title: Pace Calculation
problem: A runner finishes 5 km in 25 minutes. What is the pace in min/km?
cheatsheet: Pace = Time / Distance. (Not speed!)
steps: Pace = 25 minutes / 5 km = 5 min per km

title: Relative Speed Around a Track
problem: On a circular track (400m), Runner A at 8 m/s and B at 6 m/s start together. When does A lap B?
cheatsheet: Lap time = circumference / (faster speed - slower speed).

title: Cost Per Unit Comparison
problem: Brand A: $4.50 for 500g. Brand B: $7.20 for 800g. Which is cheaper per gram?
cheatsheet: Compare unit prices: cost/weight for each brand.
steps: They are the same price per gram

title: Basic Probability
problem: A die is rolled once. What is P(rolling a 3 or 5)?
cheatsheet: P(A or B) = P(A) + P(B) for mutually exclusive events.

title: Permutation
problem: How many ways to arrange 4 books on a shelf from a set of 7?

title: Combination
problem: How many ways to choose 3 students from a class of 10?

title: Independent Events
problem: Flip a coin and roll a die. What is P(heads AND even number)?
cheatsheet: P(A and B) = P(A) x P(B) for independent events.

title: Complement Rule
problem: What is the probability of NOT getting a 6 on a standard die?
cheatsheet: P(not A) = 1 - P(A).

title: Drawing Without Replacement
problem: Draw 2 cards from a standard deck. What is P(both are aces)?
cheatsheet: P = (4/52) x (3/51) for sequential draws without replacement.

title: At Least One
problem: Roll a die 3 times. What is P(at least one 6)?
cheatsheet: P(at least one) = 1 - P(none). Use complement rule.

title: Arrangements with Repetition
problem: How many distinct arrangements of the word MISSISSIPPI?
cheatsheet: Total arrangements = n!/(n1! x n2! x ...) for repeated letters.

title: Binomial Probability
problem: A coin is flipped 5 times. What is P(exactly 3 heads)?
cheatsheet: P = C(n,k) x p^k x (1-p)^(n-k) where n=5, k=3, p=0.5.

title: Fundamental Counting Principle
problem: A meal has 3 appetizers, 5 main courses, and 4 desserts. How many possible meals?
cheatsheet: Multiply the number of choices for each independent decision.

title: P(A or B) with Overlap
problem: In a class, 12 play soccer, 8 play basketball, 5 play both. What is P(playing at least one sport) if there are 30 students?
cheatsheet: P(A or B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A and B).

title: Expected Value
problem: A lottery ticket costs $2. You have 1% chance to win $100. What is the expected value?
cheatsheet: EV = sum of (probability x outcome) for all outcomes.

title: Conditional Probability
problem: A bag has 5 red and 3 blue marbles. Two are drawn without replacement. P(both red)?
cheatsheet: P(both red) = P(1st red) x P(2nd red | 1st was red).
steps: After removing a red: 4 red, 3 blue = 7 total

title: Odds vs Probability
problem: The probability of rain is 0.2. What are the odds against rain?
cheatsheet: Odds against = (1-P)/P. If P=0.2, odds against = 0.8/0.2 = 4:1.

title: Permutation with Identical Items
problem: How many ways to arrange 3 red and 2 blue flags in a row?
cheatsheet: n!/(r! x b!) where n=5 total flags.

title: Birthday Problem
problem: In a room of 23 people, what is P(at least two share a birthday)? (Use the approximation)
workedExample: P(no shared) is approximately 0.4927. P(shared) = approximately 50.7%.

title: Combination with Slots
problem: From 12 people, choose a president, VP, and treasurer. How many ways?
cheatsheet: This is a PERMUTATION (order matters): P(12,3) = 12 x 11 x 10.

title: Two Dice Probability
problem: Roll two dice. What is P(sum = 7)?
cheatsheet: List all combinations that sum to 7: (1,6),(2,5),(3,4),(4,3),(5,2),(6,1) = 6 out of 36.

title: Area of a Circle
problem: Find the area of a circle with radius 14 cm.
cheatsheet: Area = pi x r^2 = pi x 14^2 = 196*pi.
workedExample: A = pi x 196 = 196*pi is approximately 615.75 cm^2

title: Heron's Formula
problem: Find the area of a triangle with sides 5, 6, and 7.
cheatsheet: s = (a+b+c)/2. Area = sqrt(s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)).
workedExample: s = 9. A = sqrt(9x4x3x2) = sqrt(216) = 6*sqrt(6) is approximately 14.7

title: Pythagorean Theorem
problem: Find the hypotenuse of a right triangle with legs 9 and 12.

title: Volume of a Cylinder
problem: Find the volume of a cylinder with radius 5 and height 12.
cheatsheet: V = pi x r^2 x h
workedExample: V = pi x 25 x 12 = 300*pi is approximately 942.48
steps: V = pi x r^2 x h

title: Surface Area of a Sphere
problem: Find the surface area of a sphere with radius 5.
cheatsheet: SA = 4 x pi x r^2
workedExample: SA = 4 x pi x 25 = 100*pi is approximately 314.16

title: Area of a Sector
problem: Find the area of a sector with central angle 90 degrees in a circle of radius 10.
cheatsheet: Area = (theta/360) x pi x r^2
workedExample: A = (90/360) x pi x 100 = 25*pi is approximately 78.54

title: Volume of a Cone
problem: Find the volume of a cone with radius 4 and height 9.
cheatsheet: V = (1/3) x pi x r^2 x h
workedExample: V = (1/3) x pi x 16 x 9 = 48*pi is approximately 150.80
steps: V = (1/3) x pi x r^2 x h

title: Area of a Trapezoid
problem: Find the area of a trapezoid with parallel sides 10 and 16, height 7.
cheatsheet: A = (1/2)(a + b) x h where a and b are parallel sides.

title: Diagonal of a Rectangle
problem: Find the diagonal of a rectangle 8 x 15.
cheatsheet: d = sqrt(l^2 + w^2) by Pythagorean theorem.

title: Surface Area of a Cylinder (Total)
problem: Find the total surface area of a cylinder with radius 3 and height 10.
cheatsheet: SA = 2*pi*r^2 + 2*pi*r*h = 2*pi*r*(r+h)

title: Arc Length
problem: Find the arc length for a 120-degree angle in a circle of radius 6.
cheatsheet: Arc length = (theta/360) x 2*pi*r

title: Volume of a Pyramid
problem: Find the volume of a square pyramid with base 6 and height 8.
cheatsheet: V = (1/3) x base_area x height = (1/3) x s^2 x h

title: Perimeter of Composite Shape
problem: A rectangle 10 x 6 with a semicircle on one 10-unit side. Find the perimeter.
cheatsheet: Perimeter = 3 sides of rectangle + semicircle arc = 6 + 10 + 6 + pi x 5.

title: Distance Formula
problem: Find the distance between (1, 2) and (5, 6).

title: Slope of a Line
problem: Find the slope between points (2, 3) and (8, 15).

title: Volume of Composite Solid
problem: A cylinder (r=3, h=5) has a hemisphere on top. Find total volume.

title: Area Between Shapes
problem: Find the area of a ring (annulus) with outer radius 8 and inner radius 5.
cheatsheet: Area = pi x (R^2 - r^2)

title: Area of a Rhombus
problem: Find the area of a rhombus with diagonals 10 and 14.
cheatsheet: Area = (d1 x d2) / 2

title: Similar Triangle Sides
problem: Triangle ABC ~ DEF. AB=6, DE=9, BC=10. Find EF.
cheatsheet: Corresponding sides are proportional: AB/DE = BC/EF.

title: Solving Linear Equations
problem: Solve: 5x - 3 = 2x + 9
cheatsheet: Move variables to one side, constants to the other. Combine like terms.

title: Factoring Quadratics
problem: Factor: x^2 + 7x + 12
cheatsheet: Find two numbers that multiply to the constant term and add to the middle coefficient.
steps: Need numbers that multiply to 12 and add to 7

title: Expanding Expressions
problem: Expand and simplify: (2x + 3)(x - 5)
cheatsheet: FOIL: First, Outer, Inner, Last. Then combine like terms.

title: Simplifying Fractions
problem: Simplify: (x^2 - 4) / (x^2 + 5x + 6)
cheatsheet: Factor numerator and denominator, then cancel common factors.

title: Solving with Quadratic Formula
problem: Solve: 2x^2 + 5x - 3 = 0

title: Solving Systems by Substitution
problem: Solve: y = 2x + 1 and 3x + y = 11
cheatsheet: Substitute one equation into the other, solve for one variable, then back-substitute.

title: Difference of Squares
problem: Factor: 9x^2 - 25

title: Completing the Square
problem: Complete the square: x^2 + 6x + ?
cheatsheet: Take half of the x coefficient, square it. (b/2)^2

title: Solving Inequalities
cheatsheet: Distribute, combine, isolate x. FLIP the inequality when dividing by a NEGATIVE number.

title: Factoring by Grouping
problem: Factor: x^3 + 2x^2 + 3x + 6
cheatsheet: Group terms in pairs, factor out GCF from each pair, then factor common binomial.

title: Simplifying Radicals
problem: Simplify: sqrt(72)
cheatsheet: Find the largest perfect square factor, then split: sqrt(a x b) = sqrt(a) x sqrt(b).

title: Adding Rational Expressions
problem: Simplify: 1/(x+1) + 1/(x-1)
cheatsheet: Find common denominator, combine numerators, simplify.

title: Exponent Rules
problem: Simplify: (2x^3 y^2)^3 / (4x^2 y)^2
cheatsheet: (ab)^n = a^n b^n. (a^m)^n = a^(mn). a^m/a^n = a^(m-n).

title: Solving Absolute Value Equations
problem: Solve: |3x - 2| = 7
cheatsheet: Set up two equations: (expression) = value AND (expression) = -value.

title: Solving Rational Equations
cheatsheet: Multiply through by the LCD to clear fractions, then solve.

title: Quadratic Inequality
problem: Solve: x^2 - 4x + 3 < 0
cheatsheet: Factor, find roots, test intervals.
workedExample: (x-1)(x-3)<0. Roots: 1, 3. Test: between roots is negative. Answer: 1 < x < 3

title: Logarithmic Equation
problem: Solve: log_2(x+3) = 4
cheatsheet: Convert to exponential form: log_b(x) = n means b^n = x.

title: Perfect Square Trinomial
problem: Factor: x^2 - 10x + 25
cheatsheet: a^2 - 2ab + b^2 = (a-b)^2. Check if it fits this pattern.

title: Cube of a Binomial
problem: Expand: (x + 2)^3

title: Simple Interest
problem: Find the simple interest on $3000 at 5% for 4 years.
cheatsheet: I = P x r x t (Principal x Rate x Time)
steps: I = P x r x t

title: Compound Interest
problem: Find the amount after 3 years: $2000 at 6% compounded annually.
cheatsheet: A = P(1 + r/n)^(nt). For annual: A = P(1+r)^t

title: Monthly Compound Interest
problem: $5000 at 8% compounded monthly for 2 years. Final amount?

title: Loan Payment (Fixed Rate)
problem: $150,000 loan at 4.5% for 30 years. Approximate monthly payment?
cheatsheet: M = P[r(1+r)^n]/[(1+r)^n-1] where r = monthly rate, n = total months

title: Return on Investment
problem: Invested $10,000, sold for $13,500 after 2 years. What is the ROI?
cheatsheet: ROI = (Gain / Cost) x 100. Annualized = (1 + ROI)^(1/years) - 1.

title: Break-Even Analysis
problem: Fixed costs: $5000. Variable cost per unit: $8. Selling price: $20. Break-even quantity?
cheatsheet: Break-even = Fixed Costs / (Selling Price - Variable Cost per Unit)
steps: Need to sell at least 417 units

title: Inflation Adjustment
problem: A $50 item costs $65 after 5 years due to inflation. What was the annual inflation rate?
cheatsheet: Future = Present x (1+r)^t. Solve for r.

title: Tax Calculation
problem: Income $75,000. Tax bracket: 10% on first $10,000, 15% on $10,001-$40,000, 25% on remainder. Tax owed?
cheatsheet: Calculate tax for each bracket separately, then sum.

title: Present Value
problem: What is the present value of $10,000 received in 5 years at 7% discount rate?
cheatsheet: PV = FV / (1+r)^t

title: Rule of 72
problem: At 6% annual return, approximately how long to double your money?
cheatsheet: Years to double is approximately 72 / interest rate.

title: Currency Conversion
problem: Convert $500 USD to EUR if 1 USD = 0.92 EUR.
cheatsheet: Multiply by the exchange rate.

title: Salary to Hourly
problem: Convert a $52,000 annual salary to an hourly rate (40 hrs/week, 52 weeks).
cheatsheet: Hourly = Annual / (40 x 52)

title: Depreciation (Straight-Line)
problem: Equipment costs $20,000, salvage value $2,000, useful life 5 years. Annual depreciation?
cheatsheet: Annual depreciation = (Cost - Salvage) / Useful Life

title: Future Value of Annuity
problem: Save $200/month for 10 years at 6% annual return. Future value?
cheatsheet: FV = PMT x [((1+r)^n - 1) / r] where r is monthly rate, n is total months.

title: Discount Rate on Sale
problem: List price $250, sale price $200. What is the discount percentage?
cheatsheet: Discount % = (List - Sale) / List x 100

title: Celsius to Fahrenheit
problem: Convert 37 degrees Celsius to Fahrenheit.

title: Fahrenheit to Celsius
problem: Convert 212 degrees Fahrenheit to Celsius.

title: Pounds to Kilograms
problem: Convert 165 pounds to kilograms. (1 lb = 0.4536 kg)
cheatsheet: Multiply pounds by 0.4536 to get kilograms.

title: Miles to Kilometers
problem: Convert 26.2 miles (marathon) to kilometers. (1 mi = 1.609 km)
cheatsheet: Multiply miles by 1.609 to get kilometers.

title: Gallons to Liters
problem: Convert 15 gallons to liters. (1 gal = 3.785 L)
cheatsheet: Multiply gallons by 3.785.

title: Feet to Meters
problem: Convert 6 feet 2 inches to meters. (1 ft = 0.3048 m)
cheatsheet: Convert to total feet first, then multiply by 0.3048.

title: Inches to Centimeters
problem: Convert 48 inches to centimeters. (1 in = 2.54 cm)
cheatsheet: Multiply inches by 2.54.

title: Square Feet to Square Meters
problem: Convert 500 sq ft to square meters. (1 sq ft = 0.0929 sq m)
cheatsheet: Multiply by 0.0929 for area conversion.

title: MPH to KPH
problem: Convert 65 mph to km/h. (1 mph = 1.609 km/h)
cheatsheet: Multiply by 1.609.

title: Ounces to Grams
problem: Convert 16 ounces to grams. (1 oz = 28.35 g)
cheatsheet: Multiply ounces by 28.35.
